description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a solid, metallic head. The handle is made from a light brown wood, ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip, and tapers slightly towards the end. The axe head is forged from steel, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end, suitable for splitting or chopping tasks. The blade edge is straight and sharp, with no visible decorative elements or engravings. The head is securely mounted onto the handle using a traditional wedged fitting, ensuring stability during use. This tool is commonly found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds for cutting wood or similar materials.
description: A sturdy hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad steel blade, designed for chopping or splitting tasks. The head is securely wedged onto the handle, making it suitable for urban utility work.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad steel blade.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the ergonomic curve of the handle and the thickness of the axe head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the straight alignment of the handle and the flat poll of the axe head.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the blade's edge profile and the mounting of the head onto the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle, ergonomic, smooth finish, oval cross-section, no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a straight edge and flat poll, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fitting securing the blade to the handle.
